Title: Claude Code Best Practices \ Anthropic
Claude Code is a command line tool for agentic coding. Many engineers use `#` frequently to document commands, files, and style guidelines while coding, then include `CLAUDE.md` changes in commits so team members benefit as well. By default, Claude Code requests permission for any action that might modify your system: file writes, many bash commands, MCP tools, etc. * **Use the`/permissions`command** after starting Claude Code to add or remove tools from the allowlist. Use Claude with bash tools. Putting the above content into `.claude/commands/fix-github-issue.md` makes it available as the `/project:fix-github-issue` command in Claude Code. Claude Code doesn’t impose a specific workflow, giving you the flexibility to use it how you want. 1. **Ask Claude to read relevant files, images, or URLs**, providing either general pointers ("read the file that handles logging") or specific filenames ("read logging.py"), but explicitly tell it not to write any code just yet. 3. **Ask Claude to implement its solution in code**. When onboarding to a new codebase, use Claude Code for learning and exploration. Researchers and data scientists at Anthropic use Claude Code to read and write Jupyter notebooks. There are no required prompts or workflows, but a workflow we recommend is to have Claude Code and a `.ipynb` file open side-by-side in VS Code. * **Ask Claude to make a plan** before coding. For large tasks with multiple steps or requiring exhaustive solutions—like code migrations, fixing numerous lint errors, or running complex build scripts—improve performance by having Claude use a Markdown file (or even a GitHub issue!) as a checklist and working scratchpad:. * **Pipe into Claude Code** (e.g., `cat foo.txt | claude`), particularly useful for logs, CSVs, and large data. For example, you can pipe in a log file, then tell Claude to use a tool to pull in additional context to debug the logs. For example, the public Claude Code repository uses Claude to inspect new issues as they come in and assign appropriate labels. Have one Claude write code; use another Claude to verify. 1. Use Claude to write code. `claude -p` (headless mode) integrates Claude Code programmatically into larger workflows while leveraging its built-in tools and system prompt.

# Claude Code Batch Processing. Claude Code users often face the challenge of "wanting to execute multiple prompts sequentially" and "automating code generation with overnight batch processing." Many also need "project-specific prompt management" for their development workflows. This article provides a detailed explanation of the batch processing features added in Prompt Scheduler v1.0.2, along with real development scenarios. I want to clearly state that this is an independent personal development project with no connection whatsoever to Anthropic's Claude or Claude Code. Batch processing in Prompt Scheduler refers to **the functionality of pre-defining multiple prompts and automatically executing them in a specified order**. * Continuous prompt execution in active development projects. # Overnight batch (aggressive execution ignoring limits) \ --prompt-file \ --mode \ --ignore-approaching-limit \ --hours # Phased execution during business hours \ --prompt-file \ --mode \ --stop-at. * **Project management**: Parallel processing of multiple projects with `--prompt-file`. * **Limit processing**: Aggressive execution with `--ignore-approaching-limit`.

# Claude Code Batch Processing Complete Guide: /batch Command and Headless Mode in Practice¶. The `/batch` command in Claude Code shines when you need to apply the same transformation across hundreds of files. | Aspect | /batch Command | Headless Mode (claude -p) |. `/batch` is a slash command executed within an interactive Claude Code session1. ## Headless Mode (claude -p): CI/CD and Script Integration¶. claude -p "Summarize this file" --output-format text. claude -p "Process this large dataset" --output-format stream-json`. claude -p "Analyze this code" --output-format json | jq -r '.result'. claude -p "Summarize this file" --output-format json | jq '.usage'`. session_id=$(claude -p "Start review" --output-format json \. claude -p "Continue the review" --resume "$session_id"`. claude -p "Review the code" \. - claude -p "Analyze maintainability issues" --output-format json. - claude -p "Check for performance bottlenecks" --output-format json. Claude Code's batch processing is built on two pillars: `/batch` and headless mode (`claude -p`). Claude Code v2.1.63 Release Notes — `/batch` and `/simplify` were introduced in this version.

We use cookies to deliver and improve our services, analyze site usage, and if you agree, to customize or personalize your experience and market our services to you. # Batch processing. Instead of processing requests one at a time with immediate responses, batch processing allows you to submit multiple requests together for asynchronous processing. The Message Batches API is Anthropic's first implementation of this pattern. # Message Batches API. The Message Batches API is a powerful, cost-effective way to asynchronously process large volumes of Messages requests. ## How the Message Batches API works. When you send a request to the Message Batches API:. Any request that you can make to the Messages API can be included in a batch. Since each request in the batch is processed independently, you can mix different types of requests within a single batch. ## How to use the Message Batches API. A Message Batch is composed of a list of requests to create a Message.
